text: Just saw Rise of Skywalker. It’s fine, I guess. Decent end.
Joaquin Phoenix's performance in Joker is utterly riveting.
Did The Rise of Skywalker just forget half its plot threads? I’m honestly puzzled by some of these choices. Thoughts?

Yo, did anyone else roll their eyes when Captain Marvel just magically overpowered everything in that final fight scene? Like, really, no struggle at all? C’mon, couldn’t they at least make it a little challenging for her?

Hey, did anyone else watch The Rise of Skywalker and wonder if the makers even GET what Star Wars is about? Like, HELLO, is the message of hope and redemption just TOO HARD to grasp?! I’m rolling my eyes so hard over here! How can you fumble such epic themes with all that budget?! WOW!

Honestly, Joker’s plot is just a sad clown remix of every “society made me bad” trope. Y’all acting like it’s deep, but it’s basically a whiny dude’s spiral with extra face paint. Wake up, it’s not that profound.

Oh, come on, did you even WATCH Joker? It’s leagues above any Marvel snoozefest! The raw emotion and depth just CRUSH those cookie-cutter superhero flicks. Get real, this isn’t even a contest—Joker SLAYS! Wow!

Look, I get that Joker is supposed to be this deep, tragic dive into mental illness and society’s failures, but honestly, it’s just a slow burn of a guy moping until he snaps. The “profound” plot is overhyped—feels like a gritty fanfic for people who think chaos is automatically deep. Eye roll.
Hey, moron, if you think Joker’s subway scene isn’t pure genius, you’re blind. The tension, the chaos—it’s raw. Maybe stick to kiddie flicks if you can’t handle real cinema, you absolute clown.
How did they mess up Rise of Skywalker so bad? Plot’s a total disaster, just lazy writing for dumb fanboys! Thoughts?
That Captain Marvel crash scene? Utterly pathetic garbage!

Yo, u seriously liked that Captain Marvel trash? Ngl, it’s just woke propaganda shoved down our throats. Tbh, it’s an insult to real fans. Ur taste is straight garbage if u think this pandering crap is empowering. Lol.
